text: Risk management is an important process for any project or business. It starts with identifying risks, which means looking for potential problems that could cause delays, extra costs, or other issues. Once risks are identified, you can create mitigation strategies to reduce their likelihood or impact. For example, you might add extra time to a schedule or improve safety procedures. Despite these efforts, some risks may still happen. That is why you also need contingency plans—ready-to-use solutions if a risk becomes a real problem. Finally, risk management does not end there. You must continuously monitor risks and your plans, adjusting them as the situation changes. This ongoing process helps protect a project’s success.
